factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in utility customer spending on grid modernization and renewable energy infrastructure.

evidence.text: Revenues increased primarily due to a $1.16 billion increase in revenues from our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment, largely due to growth in spending by our utility customers on grid modernization resulting in increased demand for our electric power services and approximately $245 million of revenues attributable to acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating due to increased spending on grid modernization and renewable energy infrastructure.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions and Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions.

evidence.text: Gross profit as a percentage of revenues increased due to improved utilization and fixed cost absorption from our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions and Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions segments.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, improving profitability.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, particularly in the Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment, excluding acquisitions.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the year ended December 31, 2021 was primarily due to growth in spending by our utility customers on grid modernization, resulting in increased demand for our electric power services, as well as approximately $245 million of revenues attributable to acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, indicating strong market demand.

factors.summary: Unit volumes or transaction counts are growing, driven by increased demand from utility and industrial customers.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the year ended December 31, 2021 was primarily due to growth in spending by our utility customers on grid modernization, resulting in increased demand for our electric power services, as well as approximately $245 million of revenues attributable to acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing due to increased demand from utility and industrial customers.

factors.summary: End markets for renewable energy infrastructure and underground utility solutions are growing fastest.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the year ended December 31, 2021 was primarily due to a $450 million increase in revenues attributable to acquired businesses, primarily Blattner, the results of operations of which were included since the acquisition date of October 13, 2021. The remaining increase is due to an overall increase in transmission and interconnection construction services related to the renewable energy infrastructure.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: End markets for renewable energy and underground utilities are growing rapidly.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, particularly in compensation expenses associated with higher incentive compensation and salaries.

evidence.text: The increase in selling, general and administrative expenses was primarily attributable to a $95.6 million increase in expenses associated with acquired businesses and a $31.2 million increase in the provision for credit losses, the majority of which related to the recognition of the provision for credit loss related to a customer that declared bankruptcy in July 2021, and its affiliate.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, which could pressure margins.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to improved performance and higher revenues contributing to fixed cost absorption.

evidence.text: Gross profit as a percentage of revenues increased due to improved utilization and fixed cost absorption from our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions and Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions segments.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding, indicating improved efficiency.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly in the Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment, driven by higher revenues and fixed cost absorption.

evidence.text: The increase in operating income as a percentage of revenues was primarily attributable to improved performance across the segment, and the higher revenues contributed to higher levels of fixed cost absorption and favorable contributions associated with the timing of projects and project mix.
evidence.subsection: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving, contributing to higher fixed cost absorption.

factors.summary: Management appears confident, as evidenced by increased investment in personnel and business growth.

evidence.text: Also contributing to the increase was a $31.2 million increase in compensation expense, largely associated with increased incentive compensation expense as a result of higher levels of operating performance and an increase in salaries and benefits due to increased personnel to support business growth.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's increased investment in personnel and business growth indicates confidence.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include uncertainties and challenges in the energy market and overall economy caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, which negatively impacted margins and ability to cover fixed and overhead costs.

evidence.text: Operating income was also adversely impacted during the years ended December 31, 2021 and 2020 by the uncertainties and challenges in the energy market and overall economy caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, which negatively impacted our margins and ability to cover fixed and overhead costs.
evidence.subsection: Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: COVID-19 pandemic negatively impacted margins and ability to cover costs.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange rates and economic conditions are key sensitivities impacting results.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the year ended December 31, 2021 was primarily due to a $90 million related to more favorable foreign currency exchange rates, primarily the Canadian dollar and U.S. dollar exchange rate.
evidence.subsection: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange rates and economic conditions are sensitivities with mixed impact.

factors.summary: Specific near-term catalysts include the completion of projects and the entry into new contracts, such as the interim services agreement for the Puerto Rican electric transmission and distribution system.

evidence.text: The amounts include our portion of amounts earned by integral unconsolidated affiliates and primarily relate to our portion of amounts earned by LUMA. The increase with respect to LUMA is mostly related to the completion of the steps necessary to transition operation and maintenance of the Puerto Rican electric transmission and distribution system from the owner to LUMA in mid-2021 and the entry into an interim services agreement that covers the interim services period for the project.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Project completions and new contracts are positive developments.

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share, particularly through acquisitions such as Blattner.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the year ended December 31, 2021 was primarily due to a $450 million increase in revenues attributable to acquired businesses, primarily Blattner, the results of operations of which were included since the acquisition date of October 13, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gain in market share through acquisitions like Blattner is positive.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include its ability to secure large contracts and its presence in key markets.

evidence.text: The amounts include our portion of amounts earned by integral unconsolidated affiliates and primarily relate to our portion of amounts earned by LUMA. The increase with respect to LUMA is mostly related to the completion of the steps necessary to transition operation and maintenance of the Puerto Rican electric transmission and distribution system from the owner to LUMA in mid-2021 and the entry into an interim services agreement that covers the interim services period for the project.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Competitive advantages in securing contracts and market presence are positive.

factors.summary: The overall economic environment is a headwind due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on margins and ability to cover fixed costs.

evidence.text: Operating income was also adversely impacted during the years ended December 31, 2021 and 2020 by the uncertainties and challenges in the energy market and overall economy caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, which negatively impacted our margins and ability to cover fixed and overhead costs.
evidence.subsection: Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: COVID-19 pandemic negatively impacted economic conditions.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are impacting results, particularly in the Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the year ended December 31, 2021 was primarily due to a $90 million related to more favorable foreign currency exchange rates, primarily the Canadian dollar and U.S. dollar exchange rate.
evidence.subsection: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Favorable foreign currency exchange rates positively impacted results.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are creating growing demand for the company's products, particularly in renewable energy and underground utility solutions.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the year ended December 31, 2021 was primarily due to a $450 million increase in revenues attributable to acquired businesses, primarily Blattner, the results of operations of which were included since the acquisition date of October 13, 2021. The remaining increase is due to an overall increase in transmission and interconnection construction services related to the renewable energy infrastructure.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates growing demand for the company's products due to construction and infrastructure spending, particularly in renewable energy.

factors.summary: The company is increasing capital expenditures, particularly related to acquisitions and integration costs.

evidence.text: The increase in corporate and non-allocated costs during the year ended December 31, 2021 included an $88.7 million increase in intangible asset amortization primarily related to the acquisition of Blattner; a $27.6 million increase in acquisition and integration costs; and a $15.6 million increase in cash incentive compensation as a result of higher levels of operating performance relative to incentive compensation targets.
evidence.subsection: Corporate and Non-Allocated Costs

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company is increasing capital expenditures for acquisitions and integration, but there is no clear indication of positive or negative outcomes from this spending.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is increasing due to higher levels of debt associated with acquisitions.

evidence.text: Interest and other financing expenses increased primarily due to higher levels of debt and, to a lesser extent, a higher weighted average interest rate during the year ended December 31, 2021 compared to 2020. Our long-term debt increased significantly during the year ended December 31, 2021 in connection with our acquisition of Blattner, as described in further detail in Liquidity and Capital Resources below, and we expect that our interest expense will increase during the year ending December 31, 2022 as a result of this higher level of debt.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Debt leverage is increasing due to higher levels of debt from acquisitions, which could pose financial risks.

factors.summary: M&A activity is creating value through accretive acquisitions, particularly Blattner, which is contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the year ended December 31, 2021 was primarily due to a $450 million increase in revenues attributable to acquired businesses, primarily Blattner, the results of operations of which were included since the acquisition date of October 13, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: M&A activity is creating value through accretive acquisitions, particularly Blattner, contributing to revenue growth.
